plain_message: Attorney-client privileged communications must never be written to application logs. This could destroy privilege and expose your client to liability. Remove this log statement immediately.
Client personal data in legal matters must be encrypted at rest. Add field-level encryption before storing names, contact details, or case-related personal information.
Sending case or client data to an external service without explicit client consent and a signed Data Processing Agreement violates professional responsibility rules. Block this transmission.
Legal matter tables should have a defined retention policy. Most jurisdictions require client files for 5-7 years after matter close. Add a retention or archival mechanism.
New client or matter creation should trigger a conflict-of-interest check. Ensure this flow includes a conflict check step before engagement.
Deleting records during active litigation or investigation violates eDiscovery obligations and can result in sanctions. Verify there is no litigation hold before running this deletion.
